name: Buckeyes
recipeInstructions:
PART ONE-------------------------
Combine ingredients as in making pie crust - then knead until sugar is completely absorbed (best to use hands). Form into small balls and place on a cookie sheet; refrigerate before dipping.
PART TWO-------------------------
Melt chocolate and paraffin in the top of a double boiler. Stir thoroughly, ensuring water stays hot while dipping and stirring occasionally.
Cover a cookie sheet with waxed paper. Insert a toothpick in the center of each chilled peanut butter ball and dip into melted chocolate until it resembles a buckeye. Remove from chocolate and let drip off; place on waxed paper. Once the sheet is covered, place in refrigerator. The chocolate becomes shiny as it cools. After the buckeyes are cool, rub a finger over the toothpick hole to create a smooth surface.
So there—a thing of beauty! Good Luck! HINTS! A large bowl is needed for mixing Part 1. Experiment with the size of the balls—remember the chocolate adds to the size, so keep this in mind. It's better to halve Part 2 and do it in two operations unless you have two double boilers and friendly helping hands. The buckeyes, when cooled and set up, may be boxed using wax paper between layers; refrigerated, they will keep for long periods if they last that long.
